greeting: The fires have gone cold. Thirty years I kept them burning and now look at them.
You have the look of someone still walking toward something. I envy that.
These old eyes have watched the highland road longer than most folk have been alive.
quest: Three towers along the ridge. Three braziers gone dark. If we do not light them before nightfall the darkness owns this stretch of road.
idle: The wind is picking up. That is never a good sign this high.
I can still name every stone in every tower. That is all I have left.
